# We Will Write You the Perfect Cold Email Copy

## Also we will create your cold email strategy, do ICP research, & more... 100% FREE

AI Copywriter Lead Magnet

- Full Name\*

- Your Email\*

- Phone Number\*

- Company\*

- Choose the Best Option\*


AI Mode: Use AI and URL scraping to complete the research (must have established company with a website)Manual Mode: Answer questions manually (best for companies without a lot of information on the web)

- URLs to Research\*
Input all of the URLs that we should research. Include pages that have info on your offer, social proof, how it works, etc











Input all of the URLs that we should research. Include URLs about your offer, social proof, how it works

- Website (optional)

- Who do you serve? (Your ICP)\*
Be as detailed as possible about the job titles, company size, industries, technologies, locations that you serve.











Be as detailed as possible about the job titles, company size, industries, technologies, locations that you serve.

- What do you sell? (Your Offer)\*
Exactly what product or service do you offer? What pain point does it solve? What is your unique mechanism?











Exactly what product or service do you offer? What pain point does it solve? What is your unique mechanism?

- Social Proof (Case Studies)\*
List any specific case studies. Describe their transformation. Should be related to the ICP.











List any specific case studies. Describe their transformation. Should be related to the ICP.

- Provide Additional Context (AI Mode)
Add information about your ICP, your offer, and any social proof or case studies.











Add information about your ICP, your offer, and any social proof or case studies.

- Start Research Now 🧠

# [Free Cold Email Marketing Course for 2025 (7 Hours)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DDGcd1JoJV0)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Lead Gen Jay](http://www.youtube.com/@leadgenjay)

**Likes**: 4428
**Category**: Education

## Description

```
Download the Official Toolkit & Spark Notes Here ► http://leadgenjay.com/cem

This is the most complete free cold email course for beginners ever created. It took me more than 2 months to create, and is the most unfiltered cold email training I have ever released. Bookmark this video and use the timestamps to master b2b cold outreach automation. 

Learn how to build a cold email system to generate B2B leads and get clients for your business. From lead generation automation to email deliverability, we cover step-by-step cold email strategies, including B2B marketing tactics, client acquisition, and cold email tools like Instantly AI and Clay. Discover AI personalization, omni-channel marketing, and how to scale cold email campaigns to thousands of daily emails. Whether you’re a beginner or advanced, these cold email tips will help you boost B2B sales and create a lead-generating machine for under $100. Join our free community for exclusive resources and take your email outreach to the next level

Download the Official Toolkit & Spark Notes Here: http://leadgenjay.com/cem

Low reply rates don’t always mean bad copy.

Most people keep rewriting emails…
when the real issue is elsewhere.

Templates aren’t the enemy.
They work if:
• targeting is tight
• offer makes sense
• deliverability is solid
• volume per inbox is controlled

Relevance > personalization.

Don’t judge too early
50 emails = no signal
You need enough volume (sent safely) to know what’s working.

Track the right metrics
Forget open rates. Look at:
• total replies
• positive replies
• booked calls

High replies but no interest = offer problem

Good emails are clear:
• make money
• save money
• save time

Simple loop:
Test → send → analyze replies → keep winner → repeat

Cold email improves with data, not guesses.

What made the biggest difference for you -
copy, targeting, or infrastructure? 👇

Most cold emails fail because they feel like mass blasts.

Not because of templates
but because they’re too broad and irrelevant.

The real shift:

👉 Stop chasing personalization
👉 Start focusing on relevance

What actually works:

1️⃣ Targeting > personalization
“Tight segment” beats “everyone.”
If the problem fits, the email feels personal.

2️⃣ Write like a human
Short. Simple. No marketing language.

3️⃣ Segment-based messaging
One strong email per niche > 100 custom lines

4️⃣ Natural follow-ups
New angle each time. No “just bumping this.”

5️⃣ Scale with segments
Pick a niche → test → adjust → scale

If the targeting is right,
the same email can work for thousands.

Quick check before sending:
• Does this make sense for them?
• Does it sound human?
• Would I reply?

What’s hurting your campaigns right now
spam, data, or replies? 👇

Your 25% open rate? It’s fake.

And if you’re optimizing for it, you’re building on bad data.

Why opens don’t matter:
• Apple & Google auto-load emails → fake opens
• Tracking pixels hurt deliverability
• Opens ≠ interest

What actually matters:
• replies
• positive replies
• booked calls
• inbox placement
• OOO replies (proof you hit inbox)

Quick diagnosis:

1️⃣ No OOO + no replies → Deliverability issue
Fix: turn off tracking, warm domains, clean data

2️⃣ Replies but not positive → Messaging issue
Fix: better targeting, clearer value, stronger angles

3️⃣ Positive replies but no meetings → Offer issue
Fix: lower friction, better CTA, faster follow-ups

Stop tracking opens.
Start tracking what actually drives revenue.

Questions? Drop them below.

I’ve watched the automation space for years.

In 2026, one thing is clear:
More serious operators are moving from Zapier → n8n.

Not because Zapier is bad.
It’s great for simple workflows.

But that’s the limit.

What usually happens:
You start with Zapier → connect a few tools → automate notifications.

Then your workflows grow.

You want:
• custom logic
• code inside workflows
• AI agents
• API chains
• lower costs at scale

That’s where many teams switch to n8n.

Why?

• you can run real code in workflows
• self-host and control costs
• build complex automation in one flow

Zapier is still perfect for quick, simple automations.

But if you’re building real automation infrastructure,
a lot of builders are choosing n8n.

Curious what you're using right now - Zapier or n8n?

## ![](https://businessunitprofile-cdn.trustpilot.net/businessunitprofile-consumersite/public/trustpilot-shield.svg) We use technology to protect platform integrity, but we don't fact-check reviews

While we don't verify specific claims because reviewers' opinions are their own, we may label reviews as "Verified" when we can confirm a business interaction took place. [Read more](https://help.trustpilot.com/s/article/Trustpilots-review-labels?language=en_US)

To protect platform integrity, every review on our platform—verified or not—is screened by our 24/7 automated software. This technology is designed to identify and remove content that breaches our guidelines, including reviews that are not based on a genuine experience. We recognise we may not catch everything, and you can flag anything you think we may have missed. [Read more](https://help.trustpilot.com/s/article/How-consumers-can-flag-reviews-that-breach-our-guidelines?language=en_US)

## Review summary

Based on reviews, created with AI

Reviewers overwhelmingly had a great experience with this company. Customers consistently praise the comprehensive and practical content, highlighting its effectiveness in generating leads and structuring business models. Many appreciate the exceptional value for money, often noting that the investment far exceeded their expectations. The staff, particularly Jay and his team, are frequently commended for their expertise, active involvement in the community, and dedication to helping users achieve results. The overall user experience is described as seamless and efficient, with many finding the live calls and community support to be particularly beneficial.

However, some people were dissatisfied with the service, citing issues with the "done-for-you" setup not being fully implemented as advertised, leading to delays in receiving working mailboxes. A few other people also felt that the communication from certain team members lacked professionalism and manners, creating a negative impression.See more

#### We're open to all

[Anyone](https://help.trustpilot.com/s/article/Who-can-write-a-review-and-when?language=en_US) can write a Trustpilot review. ## Description

```
Come and ask your questions to Jay Feldman (Lead Gen Jay), a lead generation & cold email expert.

He is the founder of Otter PR, a top 10 PR firm in the US, and a member of the Inc 5000.

Jay will be sharing his secret recipe on how he scaled his marketing agency to $700,000/month.

Low reply rates don’t always mean bad copy.

Most people keep rewriting emails…
when the real issue is elsewhere.

Templates aren’t the enemy.
They work if:
• targeting is tight
• offer makes sense
• deliverability is solid
• volume per inbox is controlled

Relevance > personalization.

Don’t judge too early
50 emails = no signal
You need enough volume (sent safely) to know what’s working.

Track the right metrics
Forget open rates. Look at:
• total replies
• positive replies
• booked calls

High replies but no interest = offer problem

Good emails are clear:
• make money
• save money
• save time

Simple loop:
Test → send → analyze replies → keep winner → repeat

Cold email improves with data, not guesses.

What made the biggest difference for you -
copy, targeting, or infrastructure? 👇

Most cold emails fail because they feel like mass blasts.

Not because of templates
but because they’re too broad and irrelevant.

The real shift:

👉 Stop chasing personalization
👉 Start focusing on relevance

What actually works:

1️⃣ Targeting > personalization
“Tight segment” beats “everyone.”
If the problem fits, the email feels personal.

2️⃣ Write like a human
Short. Simple. No marketing language.

3️⃣ Segment-based messaging
One strong email per niche > 100 custom lines

4️⃣ Natural follow-ups
New angle each time. No “just bumping this.”

5️⃣ Scale with segments
Pick a niche → test → adjust → scale

If the targeting is right,
the same email can work for thousands.

Quick check before sending:
• Does this make sense for them?
• Does it sound human?
• Would I reply?

What’s hurting your campaigns right now
spam, data, or replies? 👇

[![Lead Gen Jay](<Base64-Image-Removed>)](https://www.youtube.com/@leadgenjay)

Your 25% open rate? It’s fake.

And if you’re optimizing for it, you’re building on bad data.

Why opens don’t matter:
• Apple & Google auto-load emails → fake opens
• Tracking pixels hurt deliverability
• Opens ≠ interest

What actually matters:
• replies
• positive replies
• booked calls
• inbox placement
• OOO replies (proof you hit inbox)

Quick diagnosis:

1️⃣ No OOO + no replies → Deliverability issue
Fix: turn off tracking, warm domains, clean data

2️⃣ Replies but not positive → Messaging issue
Fix: better targeting, clearer value, stronger angles

3️⃣ Positive replies but no meetings → Offer issue
Fix: lower friction, better CTA, faster follow-ups

Stop tracking opens.
Start tracking what actually drives revenue.

Questions? Drop them below.

I’ve watched the automation space for years.

In 2026, one thing is clear:
More serious operators are moving from Zapier → n8n.

Not because Zapier is bad.
It’s great for simple workflows.

But that’s the limit.

What usually happens:
You start with Zapier → connect a few tools → automate notifications.

Then your workflows grow.

You want:
• custom logic
• code inside workflows
• AI agents
• API chains
• lower costs at scale

That’s where many teams switch to n8n.

Why?

• you can run real code in workflows
• self-host and control costs
• build complex automation in one flow

Zapier is still perfect for quick, simple automations.

But if you’re building real automation infrastructure,
a lot of builders are choosing n8n.

Curious what you're using right now - Zapier or n8n?

In 2026, the hardest part of cold email isn’t writing emails.
It’s clean data.

You can have great copy and a strong offer…
but if your data is bad, nothing works.

What usually happens:
→ bad leads → high bounces → damaged domains
→ wrong contacts → no replies
→ recycled databases → burned prospects

People try to save $200 on leads…
then spend $2,000 fixing burned infrastructure.

What’s actually working now:

Data stack:
• Apollo / Sales Navigator → base contacts
• Clay → enrichment + signals
• MillionVerifier / Reoon → email verification
• Manual LinkedIn checks for high-value leads

Also underrated: niche scraping.

Industries like construction, manufacturing, and local services
aren’t getting blasted by every SDR.

The teams winning treat data hygiene as a system, not a one-time task.

Want a head start?
Comment “LEADS” and I’ll share our 8M verified leads database.

Is cold email still working in 2026?

Yes. But not the way most people do it.

95% of cold email fails.
The other 5% are getting 8-12% reply rates.

What changed?

1️⃣ Infrastructure > copy
If emails land in spam, copy doesn’t matter.
Fix your deliverability BEFORE you write a single word of copy.

2️⃣ Signals > spray-and-pray
The old playbook: Buy a list. Blast everyone. Hope for the best.
The new playbook: Only reach out to people showing buying signals RIGHT NOW.

Email people when they’re showing buying signals:
job posts, funding, tech changes, hiring, engagement.

3️⃣ Multi-touch > email only
The data is clear: combining cold email + LinkedIn touches + strategic phone calls outperforms email-only by 3x.
Not 10%. Not 50%. Three times the results.

Cold email isn’t dead.
Bad cold email is.

What’s working for you right now? 👇

Most cold emails fail for one simple reason:

They’re too long.

If someone can’t read your email in ~15 seconds on their phone, it’s getting archived.

The formula that works:

Subject: ≤5 words
Body: 4–6 lines

Structure:
1️⃣ Observation about them
2️⃣ Problem they likely have
3️⃣ Outcome you’ve helped someone achieve
4️⃣ Simple question (CTA)

Rules I follow:
• No links in the first email
• Plain text only (no HTML, images, logos)
• Ask a small question, not for a 30-min call

Simple sequence:
Day 1 → short email
Day 3 → new angle
Day 7 → new angle
Day 12 → breakup email

In 2026 inboxes are full of AI-written essays.

Short emails win because they respect attention.

What do you think hurts reply rates more:
length, CTA, or targeting?

[The $8,500 Machine That Runs My Entire Business 🤖](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-8500-machine-that-runs-my-entire-business)

Most founders are still doing this: • Paying hundreds every month for cloud servers • Paying AI companies for every API call • Paying SaaS tools for things they could run themselves Meanwhile… the founders who will dominate the next few years are building their own infrastructure. In our latest video, I break down Zeus - the $8,500 supercomputer sitting in my office that’s currently generating $48K+/month and running huge parts of my business. And no… this isn’t theory. This machine is running 24/7 powering: • massive lead scraping systems • email verification for hundreds of thousands of emails • multiple n8n automation engines • lead databases with millions of records • open-source tools replacing expensive SaaS All on one machine with zero monthly hosting costs. In this video you’ll learn: ⚡ Why cloud infrastructure becomes insanely expensive once you scale automation ⚡ Why Mac Minis and typical setups can’t handle serious automation workloads ⚡ How open-source tools can replace many SaaS products you’re paying for ⚡ The exact hardware specs and parts list to build your own system And lots more! If you’re serious about AI automation, lead generation, and building real infrastructure for your business… this video will completely change how you think about your tech stack. Watch the full breakdown now and see exactly how Zeus works.

[The #1 Client Mistake That Eats Your Time and Profits](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-1-client-mistake-that-eats-your-time-and-profits)

Most entrepreneurs don’t lose because of bad clients… They lose because of scope creep. And by the time they realize it - they’re already overworked, underpaid, and stuck. Here’s how it usually plays out: You close a client. Everyone’s excited. Week 1-2 → you’re setting things up Week 3 → client asks: “Where are the ads?” “Where are the leads?” “Can we also do LinkedIn?” And suddenly… You’re doing work you never agreed to. On timelines you never committed to. For outcomes you never promised. Now you look like the problem. The real issue? It’s not the client. It’s the lack of clarity. If you don’t define the work… The client will define it for you. Here’s how you fix it (properly) Before you sell anything: 1️⃣ Define EXACTLY what you do Be specific. No vague promises. 2️⃣ Define what you DON’T do This is where most people fail. “No ads included.” “No LinkedIn outreach.” “No additional channels.” Say it clearly. 3️⃣ Set a strict timeline Week 1 → Setup Week 2 → Infrastructure Week 3 → Testing Week 4 → Launch No assumptions. No confusion. 4️⃣ Put everything in writing Contract. Agreement. Scope doc. Make it crystal clear. So when they say: “Where are the TikTok ads?” You don’t argue. You just point to the agreement. And suddenly… You’re not defending yourself. They’re adjusting their expectations. This one shift alone will: • save you hours of unnecessary work • prevent awkward client conversations • protect your margins • and make you look 10x more professional If you want the exact contract template I use to eliminate scope creep completely… Comment “Creep” below. I’ll send it over 👇

[General discussion 📣](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen?c=160b117697d244cdae1048f5c9fb428e)

[The Testing Approach That Works Better ✅](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-testing-approach-that-works-better)

A lot of people think low reply rates mean they need a better template. So they keep rewriting emails, trying new openers, new CTAs, new angles. People say templates don’t work anymore. TEMPLATES AREN’T THE ENEMY In reality, templates work fine if: - The targeting is tight - The offer makes sense - Deliverability is good - Volume per inbox is low enough You can send the same email to 2,000 people and get great results or send a “perfect” custom email and still get ignored. Relevance beats personalization. TEST WITH ENOUGH DATA Another mistake is judging a campaign too early. Sending 50 emails and getting 1 reply doesn’t mean anything. Sending a few hundred to a few thousand (depending on your setup) gives a much clearer signal. But this doesn’t mean blasting. - Keep volume per inbox low - Ramp slowly - Let the campaign run long enough to see real replies - Then decide what to change. TRACK THE RIGHT METRIC Open rate is unreliable; we discussed this here. Better metrics: - Total reply rate - Positive reply rate - Booked calls You can have a high reply rate but no interest. That usually means the copy creates curiosity but not real value. Positive replies tell you if the offer actually fits the market. That means, offer clarity matters more than clever copy. Good emails usually fall into one of these: Make money Save money Save time If the reader can’t quickly understand what they get, reply rate drops. SIMPLE TESTING CYCLE THAT WORKS - Write a few variations - Send with safe volume - Let the campaign run - Look at replies, not opens - Keep the winner - Test again Cold email improves with data, not guesses. Curious what most people here changed that made the biggest difference. Copy, targeting, or infrastructure?

[How I Built a Self-Running AI Thumbnail Machine](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/how-i-built-a-self-running-ai-thumbnail-machine)

Alright… this one changed my content game completely. I built a custom AI thumbnail engine that now creates scroll-stopping YouTube thumbnails for about $1.30 each. Not templates. Not Canva hacks. Full custom thumbnails built around proven designs. Here’s how the system works 👇 First, I built the entire workflow inside an app using Claude Code. Everything lives in one place: - script ideas - competitor research - thumbnail generation - organized media library No bouncing between 6 tools. Then I used Ampify to scrape top-performing thumbnails from competitors based on specific keywords. So instead of “guessing” what looks good… I’m reverse engineering what’s already winning. Next step was the real unlock: I trained a custom Flux LoRA model on my own face and body. Now I can generate: - confident poses - pointing at text - excited reactions - thinking / shocked expressions All photorealistic. No photoshoots. No lighting setups. For editing, I use Nano Banana Pro. Two workflows: 1️⃣ Competitor remix I take a proven thumbnail layout → swap the original person → replace them with my AI-generated version (full body swaps, not just faces). 2️⃣ From scratch Use text-to-image to create a strong base → layer in my AI-generated images → refine with image-to-image editing. Everything gets saved automatically inside my app so I can reuse assets anytime. Now let’s talk numbers: • Base image generation: ~$0.60 • 4 AI-generated versions of me: ~$0.12 • Body swap editing: ~$0.60 Total: around $1.30–$1.50 per thumbnail. Before this? $50+ per thumbnail from designers. Multiply that across multiple videos per week… You see the leverage. If you want the full breakdown of the workflow and prompts, watch the full video here

[Pivot from "Data Provider" to "Pipeline Partner" – Lead Magnet Advice?](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/subject-pivot-from-data-provider-to-pipeline-partner-lead-magnet-advice)

Hey @Jay Feldman and the community! 🚀 I’m currently running a cold email lead gen agency (SmoothScale) targeting marketing agencies (3–12 employees). The Current Situation: I’ve been using a list of 100–200 vetted leads in their specific niche as a lead magnet to get replies. It works to get them to talk, but I’ve hit a positioning wall. The Problem: I feel like offering a "list" makes me look like a simple data provider or a "list guy." When I try to pivot to a sales call or explain my actual service (filling their pipeline with qualified calls), the transition feels clunky. They just want the list and then they go silent. The Question: What would be a better "High-Intent" lead magnet that positions me as the expert strategist who handles the entire system, rather than just the data? I’m considering: The "Loom Audit": Recording a 3-minute breakdown of why their current outbound might be failing. The "Reverse Lead Magnet": Asking them a specific question about their capacity before offering a "Custom Sample Batch." How do you guys pivot from the "Free Value" to "Let's hop on a call to build this for you"? Would love to hear how you’re structuring magnets to ensure they lead directly into a discovery call.

[How to Make Cold Email Feel Personal Without Over-Personalizing 🎯](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/how-to-make-cold-email-feel-personal-without-over-personalizing)

Most cold emails fail because they look like mass blasts. Not because the template exists, but because the targeting is too broad and the message isn’t relevant. The best cold emailers don’t write every email from scratch. They make the email feel personal by having strong segmentation, clean infrastructure, and simple copy. Here’s the shift that actually works. THE FOUNDATIONAL SHIFT Stop thinking about cold email as personalization. Start thinking about relevance. You don’t need every email to be unique. You need every email to make sense for the person receiving it. 1. TARGETING MATTERS MORE THAN PERSONALIZATION Instead of researching every prospect, tighten the segment. Bad: Marketing agencies Better: SEO agencies with 5-20 employees in the US Best emails feel personal because the problem already fits the segment. If the targeting is right, the same email can work for thousands of people. 2. WRITE LIKE A HUMAN Most cold emails fail because they sound like marketing. - Use simple language. - Short sentences. - No buzzwords. Bad: I wanted to reach out regarding our solution Better: Quick question Bad: Schedule a call to discuss Better: Want me to send it over? Cold email should sound like one person emailing another, not a campaign. 3. USE SEGMENTED PERSONALIZATION, NOT FAKE PERSONALIZATION You don’t need a custom line for every lead. You need the message to match the situation they’re in. Example structure: Opener → relevant to their segment Bridge → why you reached out Value → what they get CTA → simple next step Example: Saw you’re hiring SDRs right now. Usually that means pipeline isn’t consistent yet. We help B2B teams fix that with outbound. Want me to show you what we’re doing for similar companies? No deep research needed. Just the right segment. 4. KEEP VARIABLES NATURAL Variables are fine if the email still sounds human. The variable should fit naturally in the sentence. 5. FOLLOW UP LIKE A NORMAL PERSON Most people send robotic follow-ups.

[The AI Personalization Hack That 3X Our Cold Email Replies](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-ai-personalization-hack-that-3x-our-cold-email-replies)

Most “personalized” cold emails aren’t actually personalized. They say things like: “Saw you’re the Head of Marketing at X company.” That’s not personalization. That’s just LinkedIn scraping from 2016. Real personalization references something the person actually said. And AI makes this surprisingly easy. Here’s the workflow we’ve been using that tripled our reply rate. The AI Cold Email Personalization Workflow Step 1 - Scrape their LinkedIn activity Use tools like Apify to pull: • their last 2-3 LinkedIn posts • engagement text • sometimes even comments Now you have real signals about what they care about. Step 2 - Feed those posts into AI Take the posts and send them to GPT with a structured prompt. Now your email starts with something like: "Noticed your Series B announcement - we recently helped a similar company handle \[specific challenge\] while expanding across multiple countries." That instantly feels human. Step 3 - Generate personalized openers at scale Instead of manually researching every prospect… AI reads the posts → extracts insights → writes the opening line. Now every email feels like you actually read their content, even when you’re sending at scale. Why this works Most cold emails fail in the first sentence. People can instantly tell if: • it’s generic • it’s templated • it’s automated But when you reference something they posted… It breaks the pattern. And that’s when conversations start. If you want to learn more about cool AI + automation systems like this (the kind we use inside our outbound and lead gen machines)… Comment “AIA”

[Cold Truth: Opens + OOO ≠ Success](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/cold-truth-opens-ooo-success)

Your 25% open rate? It's fake. And if you're optimizing based on it, you're building your entire cold email strategy on broken data. Why Open Rates Don't Matter: - Apple Mail Privacy Protection - Auto-opens emails to preload images. You see "opens" even if nobody read it. - Google Image Proxy - Loads images through proxies, triggering fake opens. - Tracking Pixels Hurt Deliverability - That open tracking pixel makes you look like marketing spam, hurting inbox placement. - Opens ≠ Interest - Someone can open and delete instantly. Someone can never "open" but still reply. Opens tell you nothing about whether your campaign is actually working. The Metrics That Actually Matter - Out-of-office replies (proof you hit the inbox) - Total replies - Positive reply rate - Booked calls - Inbox placement tests The Real Diagnostic Framework 1️⃣ No OOO + No Replies = Deliverability Problem You're not reaching the inbox. Fix it: - Turn OFF open tracking - Turn OFF link tracking - Warm your domain properly (start slow) - Fix SPF/DKIM/DMARC - Clean your list - Run inbox placement tests 2️⃣ Getting OOO + Replies But Nothing Positive = Messaging Problem You're landing in the inbox, but nobody cares. Fix it: - Re-validate your targeting (right people?) - Lead with THEIR pain, not YOUR solution - Make value prop specific and quantifiable - Add real proof - Test 3-5 different angles 3️⃣ Getting Positive Replies But No Meetings = Offer Problem Your positioning works, but your ask is wrong. Fix it: - Lower time commitment (15 min, not 60) - Frame meeting around their outcome - Respond fast - Reduce friction Stop tracking opens. Start tracking replies. Use the framework above to diagnose what's broken, then fix THAT before touching anything else. Questions? Drop them below.

[The Lead Data Crisis 🤯](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-lead-data-crisis)

In 2026, the hardest part of cold email isn't writing emails. It's finding clean data. You can have the best copywriter in the world. The perfect subject line. A killer offer. None of it matters if your data is garbage. And right now? Most people's data IS garbage. Here's what I see over and over again: → Bad lead data → high bounce rates → domain damage → Wrong contacts (you're emailing the office manager, not the decision maker) → Overlapping databases (Apollo + Apollo + Apollo = the same burned contacts) → Cheap scraped lists with zero verification People try to save $200/month on leads… then spend $2,000 replacing burned domains and rebuilding infrastructure. Good data is expensive. Burned infrastructure is MORE expensive. Let that sink in. Here's what's actually working right now: The people getting results aren't relying on one tool. They're running a data hygiene stack: - Base Data: Apollo / Sales Navigator → starting point for contacts - Clay → layering in job titles, company size, tech stack, intent signals - Verification: MillionVerifier / Reoon → cleaning out invalid emails BEFORE they hit your sender (btw you can build your own email verifier, check this) - Google / LinkedIn check → for high-value prospects, verify manually - Custom Scraping: For niche industries where databases are actually clean The niche scraping play is underrated. Here's why: Industries like local services, manufacturing, construction, and geo-technical companies aren't getting blasted by every SDR with an Apollo login. Those databases are cleaner because the competition isn't there. Everyone's fighting over the same SaaS founders and marketing directors. Meanwhile, the people targeting old-school industries are getting 10%+ reply rates because their leads haven't been emailed 47 times this month. The real cost breakdown nobody talks about: Most people think cold email costs = software + sending tool.

[One CTA change tripled our cold email reply rates. 🤯](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/one-cta-change-tripled-our-cold-email-reply-rates)

We stopped asking for calls in cold email. Reply rates tripled. The problem was never the subject line. Never the personalization. Never the send time. It was the CTA. "Book a 15-minute call" is the most selfish ask in sales. Think about it from the prospect's side. They get 40-60 cold emails a week. Every single one ends with a calendar link. Why would they say yes? They won't. And they don't. Here's the principle I've built my entire outbound system around: Value before velocity. The goal of a cold email is not to book a meeting. The goal is to earn the right to ask for one. So instead of ending with a calendar link, we end with a deliverable. Here's exactly what that looks like in practice: First - The Competitor Intelligence Report We use AI to build a 1-page breakdown of the prospect's top 3 competitors: their positioning, their messaging angles, their ad spend patterns, their review sentiment. We hand it to them for free in the email. No strings. No opt-in. Just value. Why it works: executives are obsessed with what competitors are doing. You've just made yourself useful before the first conversation. Second - The Outbound Audit We analyze their current cold outreach, their LinkedIn presence, their offer messaging, and their ICP targeting. We send a personalized 5-point breakdown of exactly where they're leaving money on the table. Why it works: you're not selling your service. You're demonstrating it. The audit IS the pitch. And the Final - The Micro Tool We use Claude Code to build a lightweight tool specific to their business. A scoring calculator. A proposal generator. A quick ROI estimator. Something they can actually use this week. Why it works: nothing converts faster than utility. When someone uses your tool, they're already in your ecosystem. The results we see consistently: - Reply rates 3x above industry benchmarks. - Response quality is higher (decision makers, not gatekeepers). - Sales cycles are shorter because trust is already established before call one.

[Why Most Beginners Fail at Cold Email in the First 30 Days (It Is Not the Copy)](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/why-most-beginners-fail-at-cold-email-in-the-first-30-days-it-is-not-the-copy)

I talk to a lot of people who tried cold email, got terrible results, and gave up within a month. Every time I dig into what happened, the problem is almost never the copy. Here is what it actually is. Problem 1: They used their main domain. They sent from their company email. It got flagged. Emails stopped landing in inboxes. They thought cold email did not work. It worked fine. Their setup was broken. Problem 2: They did not warm up the accounts. They bought a domain on Monday, set up an email account on Tuesday, and started sending 50 emails on Wednesday. Email providers flagged the account as suspicious within a week. Again, emails stopped landing. Same conclusion: cold email does not work. Problem 3: They built a bad list. They pulled a generic list from a database, filtered by industry and company size, and called it done. The list had no specific cohesion. The emails were sent to people with different pain points, different buying situations, different contexts. The message resonated with nobody. Problem 4: They expected results in week 2. Cold email is a 60-90 day game. The first 30 days is mostly setup and testing. You are learning what resonates, fixing deliverability issues, tightening your list criteria. Expecting meetings from week 1 is like expecting to close a sale the first time someone hears your name. Problem 5: They stopped after one follow-up. Most replies come after follow-up number 2 or 3. Sending one email and moving on leaves most of your potential meetings on the table. The frustrating thing about all five of these problems is that none of them are copy problems. You could have the best email ever written and it will not matter if your infrastructure is broken, your list is vague, or you gave up after two weeks. Fix the setup first. Write decent copy second. Then be patient. Which of these five mistakes have you made? No judgment here, most of us have made at least two of them.

[I Stopped Sending Looms. Replies Went Up.](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/i-stopped-sending-looms-replies-went-up)

Alright, I want to break down a strategy that used to work insanely well, stopped working for most people, and is now quietly working again if you apply it the right way. You’ll probably recognize this. A few years ago, Loom videos crushed. You’d send a cold email like: “Hey, mind if I record you a quick Loom?” They’d say yes. You’d spend 10 minutes recording a custom video. Send it over. Replies would spike. Why did it work? It worked because it felt 1-to-1. The prospect believed you sat down, thought about them, and made something specifically for their business. That’s the psychology. Now here’s the problem: Doing this at scale is brutal. Recording 10 minutes per prospect doesn’t scale past a handful of leads a day. So people abandoned it. Big mistake. The strategy didn’t stop working. The format did. Instead of sending a Loom, you switch the deliverable. Here’s what works now: You ask permission to do research, not a video. Something like: “Would it be useful if I put together a quick breakdown of your company vs competitors? I can send it over in 24 hours.” That’s it. What happens in their head: – “They’re going to spend time on me” And that’s the win. Now the real unlock: You don’t actually need to do this manually. With automation, you can generate: – mini audits – competitor snapshots – opportunity breakdowns – teardown-style reports All personalized, fast and scalable. From the prospect’s perspective, it feels just as personal as the old Loom videos. From your side, you’re not burning 10 minutes per lead. This is what I call a reverse lead magnet. Instead of giving away a generic PDF to everyone, you offer something that appears custom first, then deliver it at scale. If you want to see exactly how I set it up… Comment “Reverse” and I’ll break it down.

[The New Cold Email Meta](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/the-new-cold-email-meta)

I’ve been running cold email campaigns for years now. And one thing is very clear: Cold email still works. Meetings are getting booked. Deals are still closing. Pipelines are still being built. But the way most people run outbound today is completely outdated. Here are the biggest shifts we’re seeing. 1️⃣ Deliverability is now the real bottleneck Getting emails into the inbox has become exponentially harder. The old playbook was simple: Buy domains → warm them → blast emails. That approach is dying fast. Today you need to: • test inbox placement before campaigns • monitor sender reputation constantly • keep warmups running always A good rule we follow: ➡️ Send ~15-20 warm-up emails per day per inbox ➡️ Send ~15-20 real emails per day per inbox This balances your sending patterns and keeps inbox providers comfortable with your activity. 2️⃣ Segmentation has replaced “personalization” A few years ago a custom first line was impressive. Now? Everyone is doing AI personalization. And most of it is terrible. You’ve probably seen emails like: “Hey John, saw your LinkedIn post about AI.” Congratulations. You scraped LinkedIn. That’s not impressive anymore. Instead of writing a custom line for every person… We focus on segmented personalization. Meaning: Your message is written for a very specific segment where the problem is already relevant. 3️⃣ The segmentation framework we use Before writing a single email, we segment markets across five dimensions: 1️⃣ Sub-industry Example: fintech → payments → embedded finance 2️⃣ Company size Startup vs SMB vs enterprise 3️⃣ Geography Region, country, or even state 4️⃣ Company maturity Bootstrapped vs VC-backed 5️⃣ Core problem Transaction fees, reporting, payout delays, etc. Once you combine a few of these… You end up with hyper-relevant segments. 4️⃣ Match segments to the right persona Once segments are defined, map them to the right decision maker. Example: • Ecommerce brands → Head of Ecommerce / Growth

[Copywriting ✍️](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen?c=47df0183b5624b1c9292370f34ca624c)

[Cold Email Is Dead (If You're Still Writing Long Paragraphs)](https://www.skool.com/lead-gen/cold-email-is-dead-if-youre-still-writing-long-paragraphs)

Most cold emails fail for one reason - they take too long to read. If your prospect can't read your email in 15 seconds on their phone, it's getting archived. Period. Here's what changed everything for us: The 15-Second Cold Email Formula: Subject line: 5 words or less. No clickbait. Body: 4-6 lines. That's it. Here's the structure: Line 1 - Observation (something specific about THEM, not you) Line 2 - Problem (the pain they're probably feeling) Line 3 - Outcome (what you've done for someone like them) Line 4 - CTA (one low-friction question, not a calendar link) That's it. The whole email. The 3 rules that make this work: NO links in the first email. Links trigger spam filters and make it feel like marketing. Your first email should feel like a human typed it in 30 seconds. NO images, NO HTML. Plain text only. HTML formatting, logos, and signatures scream "mass email." NO asking for 30 minutes. Lower the commitment, raise the reply rate. The sending schedule that prints meetings: Day 1 - Send the 15-second email (above) Day 3 - Follow up with ONE new angle (different problem or proof point, 3-4 lines) Day 7 - New angle, same offer, different pain point Day 12 - "Breakup" email - "Figured this isn't a priority right now, totally get it. If \[problem\] comes back up, happy to help." 3 emails. That's the whole sequence. No 12-step nurture. No "just bumping this up." Why this works in 2026: Everyone's inbox is flooded with AI-generated novels disguised as cold emails. Long, "personalized" paragraphs that somehow all sound the same. The counterintuitive move is going SHORTER. When every email in their inbox is 3 paragraphs, your 4-line email stands out because it respects their time. Which part of your cold email do you think is killing your reply rate - length, CTA, or something else? Drop it below, happy to take a look.

## Here are some reasons why you should NOT join

#### **I have joined other courses and I have been burned in the past**

I hear you, me too. I’ve signed up for over 100 paid courses in my journey. My goal was to make sure you never say this about my program. That’s why I went above and beyond to pour value in, without charging crazy prices. Also, I have a 14 day refund policy if you change your mind.

#### **I am not sure the content will apply to my business**

This program is designed to help any b2b business generate leads. That ranges from agencies to SaaS companies, to local b2b service providers. If you join and you realize it's not for you, you can take advantage of the 14-day money back guarantee.

#### **I’m not sure I have the time to dedicate to the course right now**

The good news is that it's a lifetime membership, so there’s no rush. A lot of the value inside the program is done-for-you, so we can get started with that right away. Remember that we raise our prices every quarter. So, if you’re thinking about it, now is the time!

#### **I am worried the course is too advanced for me**

I designed this program to take you from total beginner to advanced. Hundreds of people have entered the program with little or no experience and now are doing $50K/mo within 90 days. You can do it too.

#### **I need a moneyback guarantee to join**

We offer a 14-day moneyback guarantee. If you join and you actually watch the course content and then decide its not for you, we will give you a full refund.

#### **I don’t have the money but I want to join**

I do not want money to be a barrier between you and your success. We offer some great financing options that start at just $49 to enroll today. Visit [leadgenjay.com/lnpl](http://leadgenjay.com/lnpl) to get financing.
